title: June Pona Lyrics Unnale Unnale
stanzas:
June pona july kaatre
kan paarthaa kadhal kaatre
poo poothaa thaen varume
pen paarthaa thee varume

ennaachu thonalaye
yedhaachu theriyalaiye
natpaachu love illaiye
love aachu natpillaiye

netru enbadhum
kaiyil illai
naalai enbathum
paiyil illai
indru mattume
nenjil micham undu.

thozha..
muththa koothukal yarukkaaga
moththa boomiyum
kooththukkaagathaan. (2)

June pona july kaatre
kan paarthaa kadhal kaatre
poo poothaa thaen varume
pen paarthaa thee varume

aaraikulle mazhai varumaa
veliye vaa kudhugalamaa
indha boomi pandhu
engal koodai pandhu
andha vaanam vandhu
koorai seithathindru.

karai irukkum nilavinai
salavai sei
sirai irukkum manangalai
paravai sei
endha malargalum
kanneer sindhi kandathillai.

iruppomaa velipadaiyaai
sirippomaa malar kudayaai
sirpi viralgalum
silai sedhukkume
pennin vizhigalo
nammai sedhukkume

romba kadhalai indha boomi
kandirukkum
pala maatrangal
vandhu vandhu poiyirukkum

kan paarthaa kadhal kaatre
poo poothaa thaen varume
pen paarthaa thee varume

ennaachu thonalaye
yedhaachu theriyalaiye
natpaachu love illaiye
love aachu natpillaiye

netru enbadhum
kaiyil illai

paiyil illai
indru mattume
nenjil micham undu.

thozha..

moththa boomiyum
kooththukkaagathaan. (2)

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of June Pona?

The song explores themes of love and longing, capturing the emotional turmoil of a relationship.

Who is the singer of June Pona?

The song is sung by Krish, known for his soulful voice.

Who composed the music for Unnale Unnale?

The music for the movie was composed by Harrish Jeyaraj.

What is the release year of Unnale Unnale?

The movie was released in 2007.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

The song reflects the protagonist's feelings of nostalgia and heartache as he reminisces about a past relationship.
enrichedMetadata.summary: June Pona is a poignant song from the movie Unnale Unnale, capturing the essence of love and loss. Sung by Krish, the melody beautifully conveys the emotions of nostalgia and longing. The lyrics reflect the protagonist's inner turmoil as he navigates the complexities of a past relationship. Harrish Jeyaraj's music adds depth to the song, making it a memorable piece that resonates with anyone who has experienced the bittersweet nature of love.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: June Pona from the movie Unnale Unnale is a captivating blend of Harrish Jeyaraj's soulful music and Krish's emotive vocals. This song beautifully captures the essence of love and longing, making it a timeless favorite. Dive into the emotional journey that this melody offers, resonating with anyone who has experienced the bittersweet nature of love.
stanzaMeanings: When June passes, the July breeze comes. When you look, love is like the breeze. When flowers bloom, honey comes. When you see a girl, fire comes.
What happened? I don't feel anything. I don't know anything. Friendship is there, but no love. Love is there, but no friendship.
Yesterday is not in my hands. Tomorrow is not in my pocket. Only today is left in my heart.
Friend, for whom are these grand shows? The whole world is just a stage.
When June passes, the July breeze comes. When you look, love is like the breeze. When flowers bloom, honey comes. When you see a girl, fire comes.
Will it rain inside? Come outside, let's jump. This earth is our ball, and the sky is our roof.
Make the moon with a shore clean. Make the minds that are trapped fly. No flowers have cried tears.
Shall we stay like a bright light? Shall we smile like a blooming flower? Sculptor's fingers carve statues, but a girl's eyes shape us.
This earth has seen a lot of love. Many changes have come and gone, but they are false.
In this world, no one is like Rama.
When June passes, the July breeze comes. When you look, love is like the breeze. When flowers bloom, honey comes. When you see a girl, fire comes.
What happened? I don't feel anything. I don't know anything. Friendship is there, but no love. Love is there, but no friendship.
Yesterday is not in my hands. Tomorrow is not in my pocket. Only today is left in my heart.
Friend, for whom are these grand shows? The whole world is just a stage.
